stat_of_week: 19 HR - BaseballPro77 slugged a league-record 19 home runs in a single week, breaking the previous mark and anchored by Mike Trout's monstrous 6-HR, 11-RBI, .486 OBP performance
thriller_note: The only tie of the week came down to a perfect deadlock across nine categories, with Canberra's modest offense meeting Riverside's decent pitching in a result that helped neither team in the standings and leaves both squads spinning their wheels in the middle of the pack.
key_storyline: BaseballPro77 delivered the most dominant offensive performance of the season, hanging 19 home runs, 39 RBI, and a 2.33 ERA on their way to a 7–2 demolition of the Wisconsin Rapids Reapers — the HR and RBI totals are new single-week league records. The engine was Mike Trout, who looked fully healthy and otherworldly with 6 HR, 11 RBI, and a .486 OBP, supported by Munetaka Murakami and Austin Riley each adding multi-HR, multi-RBI weeks. If this is what a locked-in BaseballPro77 roster looks like, the rest of the league should be very nervous.
lucky_reason: Despite posting just 4 HR, 45 K, and the fewest hits in their matchup — and being outperformed in most counting stats — the Benchwarmers carry a luck delta of +1.21, the highest in the league, meaning their actual wins vastly exceed what their underlying numbers would predict.
unlucky_reason: The Twins won their matchup 6–4 but posted a luck delta of -0.61 — the worst in the league — meaning their strong offensive week from José Ramírez (9 R, 4 HR, 5 SB, .500 OBP) and Yordan Alvarez (9 R, 4 HR) was consistently undervalued by circumstance, and their expected wins far outpace their actual credited results.
records_broken: BaseballPro77 set new single-week league records for both most HR by a team (19, breaking the previous mark of an unspecified prior high) and most RBI by a team (39), both set in their Week 4 blowout of the Wisconsin Rapids Reapers.
power_rankings: 1. BaseballPro77 | Historic offensive week with league-record HR and RBI; elite ERA and rotation depth; clear top dog | up
2. General Hospital | 8-category win total this week, dominant pitching (6 QS, 2.68 ERA, 0.96 WHIP), consistent across all categories | up
3. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dropped their matchup but still lead cumulative category wins over four weeks; deep and balanced roster | same
4. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge continues to destroy, elite pitching staff, 5 QS and solid ERA; winning the right way | up
5. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | 8 QS is a league-high this week, pitching is elite (2.49 ERA, 0.98 WHIP); offense just enough to win | up
6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | José Ramírez and Yordan Alvarez both exploded; unluckiest team per index despite a W; real upside here | up
7. North Correa | Chris Sale was filthy (0.69 ERA, 0.69 WHIP); lost on pitching ERA meltdown elsewhere but competitive roster | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Fantastic pitching (2.81 ERA, 1.09 WHIP, 5 QS) not enough to overcome negative NSVH; dangerous sleeper | same
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Byron Buxton and Sal Stewart carried the week; waiver activity suggests roster instability but scrappy enough to win | same
10. Riverside Roughnecks | Tied when they should have won; Shea Langeliers and Nick Kurtz quietly excellent; pitching ERA needs work | down
11. Canberra Wombats | Tied when they needed a full win; thin offense and limited pitching make ceiling hard to envision | same
12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got steamrolled 7–2; historically bad hit total (33 H) and anemic OBP (.257); Ben Rice a bright spot but roster is thin | down
13. Buttville Skidmarks | Lost a winnable matchup; ERA issues and low HR output; Bryan Reynolds' .500 OBP masked a broader offensive slump | same
14. Benchwarmers | Luck can't paper over a 2-category week for long; bottom-tier HR, K, and ERA; Michael King was excellent but can't do it alone | down
